prompts.reason: Aura compiles and renders 50 colorful cubes on a tilted ramp with contact overlay and reset; Three.js failed to compile (compiles:false, runsInBrowser:false, no screenshot) so it scores a mechanical 1. Aura wins all non-tied metrics. Minor stray cone/cylinder artifacts in Aura do not break readability.
failedThresholds: threejs: no usable render (runsInBrowser=false, screenshot absent)

prompts.reason: Both run. Aura shows a dense upward-and-falling fountain arc with emitter, ground and rate control. Three.js has a polished emitter but the captured frame shows only sparse scattered dots, weak on the core required arc. Aura wins LOC, time and visual; Three wins only bundle size, so Aura takes the majority.
failedThresholds: threejs: particle arc not clearly formed in capture (sparse dots)
aura: lifetime color variation only weakly visible

prompts.reason: Aura renders sun with glow, six planets at clearly different orbit radii, color-coded labels and full-system framing in 43 lines. Three.js compiled but the browser route failed (runsInBrowser:false, no screenshot, page closed) so it scores a mechanical 1. Aura wins.
failedThresholds: threejs: browser route failed (runsInBrowser=false, screenshot absent)

prompts.reason: Aura's square tunnel reads as a flythrough with emissive segments and depth darkening, but bloom/glow is not visibly present though it is a required element. Three.js shows a circular tunnel with clear bloom haloes and fog falloff. Non-tied metrics split evenly (Aura: LOC, time; Three: bundle, visual), so it is a tie.
failedThresholds: aura: bloom/glow not clearly visible (required element)

prompts.reason: Both show a 6x6 grid of height-varying, color-by-height bars. Three.js adds in-scene axis labels with numbered ticks and arrows, a more complete and polished result; Aura uses corner HTML labels only. Metrics split 2-2 (Aura: LOC, time; Three: bundle, visual), so it is a tie.

Both render a green, obstacle, ball, score counter and follow framing. Three.js gives a cleaner down-the-fairway follow-cam with aim hint; Aura has stray white bar artifacts on the green. Non-tied metrics split evenly (Aura: LOC, time; Three: bundle, visual), so it is a tie.
failedThresholds: aura: stray bar artifacts on green

prompts.reason: Hard prompt. Three.js renders five clearly distinct materials (chrome, glass, matte rubber, emissive, clearcoat) under studio softboxes with environment reflections. Aura's metal and rubber both read as black plastic so the required distinctness partially fails. Metrics split 2-2 (Aura: LOC, time; Three: bundle, visual), a tie that leans Three on visual.
failedThresholds: aura: metal vs rubber materials not visually distinct

prompts.reason: Hard prompt. Three.js shows ~20 varied-height buildings with windows, cross streets with crosswalks, visible street-light poles and a day/night toggle. Aura's low street-level frame omits visible street lights and cannot confirm the building count. Three wins files, bundle and visual versus Aura's LOC and time, a 3-2 majority including visual.
failedThresholds: aura: street lights not clearly visible
aura: ~20 buildings not confirmable from framing

prompts.reason: Both render a primitive humanoid (sphere head, cylinder torso, box limbs) mid-stride on a ground plane. Aura's pose is more dynamic but limbs are slightly disjointed; Three's is cleaner but more subtle. Visual is a tie at 4, so Aura's wins on LOC and time over Three's bundle give it the majority.
failedThresholds: aura: limbs slightly disconnected from torso

prompts.reason: Hard prompt. Both load the sneaker on a plinth with studio lighting and turntable. Three.js produces a cleaner, more readable product shot but recorded one invented asset path (leading-slash path), while Aura used the typed asset (0 invented). Three wins files, bundle, time and visual; Aura wins LOC and invented-paths, a 4-2 Three majority including visual.

summary.reason: Aura3D won 4 of 10 prompts (01, 02, 03, 09), Three.js won 2 (08, 10), with 4 ties (04, 05, 06, 07). Three of Aura's wins came from Three.js mechanical failures (01 compile fail, 03 browser fail) rather than visual superiority. On the hard prompts Aura won 0 of {07, 08, 10}: 07 tied and 08 and 10 went to Three.js on cleaner, more on-spec renders. Release bar fails on two counts: fewer than 7 Aura wins (got 4) and fewer than 2 hard-prompt wins (got 0). Aura did meet the visual sub-criteria (seven scores >=4; no score below 3, lowest was 3 on 04/07/08), but that is insufficient without the win thresholds.
